must.title: Use native <a> or role="link"
must.description: Use a native <a href> element. If a non-anchor element must act as a link, add role="link" and handle keyboard navigation manually

must.title: Link has a descriptive accessible name
must.description: The link text or aria-label must describe the destination. Avoid generic text like 'click here' or 'read more'

must.title: Enter activates the link
must.description: Pressing Enter on a focused link must execute navigation to the link target

must.title: Anchor must have a valid href
must.description: A native <a> element used as a link must have an href attribute; without it the element is not keyboard focusable by default

should.title: Warn users when opening in a new tab
should.description: When a link opens in a new tab or window, indicate this in the link text or aria-label (e.g., 'Documentation (opens in new tab)')

should.title: Visible focus indicator
should.description: Provide a clear focus style; don't remove the default outline without providing a stronger alternative

should.title: Links distinguishable from surrounding text
should.description: Links within body text should be distinguishable by more than colour alone (e.g., underline)

avoid.title: Links with empty or hash-only href
avoid.description: Avoid <a href="#"> or <a href="javascript:void(0)"> for interactive elements — use <button> instead if no navigation occurs

avoid.title: Image links without alt text
avoid.description: Don't use images inside links without meaningful alt text — the link will have no accessible name

react.label: React Link with new-tab warning

html.label: HTML Link examples
html.code: About us Example site (opens in new tab)

tests.title: Keyboard Navigation
steps: Verify navigation occurs to the expected destination

tests.title: Screen Reader Link Purpose
steps: Use screen reader link list (Insert+F7 in NVDA)
Verify each link has a unique, descriptive name that makes sense out of context
